posts.author: Aarti
posts.text: the china and india plants were set up 10 years back. not recently. india facilites were not utilised due to EDQM issues and after they got approvals they have not been able to increase utilisation after so long… even though these facilities are not new.. customers have not returned.. which is a big concern. china plant was established 10 years back and they received approval form Chinese FDA now !! Even after that utilisation has not increased. advice to anybody investing in this company is to read concall transcripts for at least 5 to 6 years.

posts.author: espl
posts.text: The plant installations are more or less done, there are no more new facilities to be added in the near term at least. The facility utilization is currently at low levels. As capacity utilization increases, the ROCE will increase gradually. The issue is that the management has been giving never ending guidelines of 2 more years. Indian EDQM was received in 2023; we are almost 3 years since the approval, France facility was also supposed to be breakeven in 2 years (Launched in FY24 as far as I remember). But now they are saying 2 more years. For Debt, I think they are not taking more debt. At a…

posts.author: Aarti
posts.text: with such low ROCE market it still giving high valuation to the company - PE of 30+ is quite high… and with everything being negative India and china not doing well plan to take more debt france facility yet to break even etc..etc.

posts.author: CHIRAG_BARASARA
posts.text: With very low profitability why continues invest outside of INDIA ? EU Manpower always been costly!! AS Per management guidelines France facilities will be profitable in next 2 years.. then what will be Payback period as it takes few years to be profitable only !! What would be management thinking behind this type of investment !! I am new to this sector

posts.author: Aarti
posts.text: the company does good work.. good people/ tech in Europe… I know the company quite well… problem is high costs in Europe.. and India/ china facilities not being utilised. they can make money in india and china but have not been able to. European facilitied incl the France new facility are very good with good customers but low margins… I doubt company will make good returns without India and China performing… which have not performed for many years now and seems unlikely now.. other companies in CDMO have grown by leaps and bounds and Dishman being one of the first in this space in india has no…

posts.author: Vysakh
posts.text: Pharma timelines are very long. From getting regulatory approvals to RFP and to actual revenue ramp up could take several years. In dishmans case new plant in france, bavla regulatory approvals, coinvestment plant for japanese customer all completed around the same time (2024 to 25). While the interest cost, Depreciation etc affect the company immediately, the actual benefits should come delayed.it should take around 3 years to show good capacity utilisation (bavla could take much longer because of loss of customer confidence due to past issues). The coinvestment plant rampup could be faster a…

posts.author: startupfundas
posts.text: Cash flow from operations nearly 491 cr, yes interest takes out a chunk (174 cr) still free cash of 217 is not to be scoffed at. Yes the capital structure seems to be the issue plus too much revenue coming from high cost sites plus France still bleeding. But seems to be progressing in right direction, albeit at a very very slow pace

posts.author: Aarti
posts.text: INR has depreciated by 20% against CHF this year. so 20% increase in topline and EBIDTA (increased by 20%) is just through currency rates not operational improvement. of course all revenue is not in CHF - even if small india revenue is removed from calculation you can clearly see that most of improvement is from currency depreciation.. company’s operation is not improving. ROCE of company is 3%. How can this be good. and for the nth time, company (Harshil Dalal, CFO) has given rosy projections for the future.. will that ever happen? SEBI should take action for giving false hopes to investors f…

posts.author: austin
posts.text: As expected topline and EBITDA came up good, overall things seem to be going in the direction as per guidance - I think now debt is the biggest issue, and probably single biggest reason for valuation as it stands.
